title: Legitimacy as a Feasibility Gate for Natural Resource Extraction
author: Bell, Peter
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Natural resources extraction politics often becomes impossible not only because people disagree about how much extraction should occur, but because they do not trust the decision process. This paper models legitimacy as a gate rather than as another issue preference. Some extraction policies cannot even be proposed or sustained unless legitimacy conditions are satisfied. The model separates three layers: legitimacy institutions (Z) determine what is politically feasible; standards and enforcement (Y) improve performance within what is feasible; and extraction intensity (X) is chosen inside the feasible region. This structure explains a distinctive minority-party strategy: bargain first for legitimacy and verification reforms that expand what is workable, then support or constrain extraction conditionally. The companion research object contains the figures, tables, and scripts needed to reproduce the model and protocol.
